title: Sangeetha Jaadthi Mullai Lyrics Kaadhal Oviyam
stanzas:
Sangeetha Jaadhi mullai
Kaana villai
Kangal vandhum
Paavai indri paarvai illai

Raagangal indri
Sangeetham illai
Saavondruthaana
Nam kadhal ellai
En naadhame vaa..

Thirumugam vandhu pazhagumo
Arimugam seidhu vilagumo
Vizhigalil thuligal vadiyumo
Adhu suduvadhai thaanga mudiyumo

Kanavinil endhan uyiril
uravaagi vidigayil indru
Azhudhu pirivaagi
Thanimayil endhan idhayam
Sarugaagi udhirumo

Vizhigal azhudhapadi
Karangal thozhudha padi
Siraigalum podipada
Velivarum oru kizhi
Isai ennum mazhai varum
Ini endhan mayil varum

niyabaga vedhanai theerumo
Aadiya paathangal
Kadhalin Vedhangal
paadidumo Aadidumo
Paadidumo aadidumo
Aadidumo ....

Raja dheepame
Endhan vaasalile vaaraayo
Kuyile.. kuyile ..
kuyile .. Kuyile ..

Naan thaedi vandha
Kodai nilavu aval
neethaane neethaane
Mana kannil nindru
Pala kavidhai thandha
Magal neethaane neethaane
.
Vizhiyillai enumpodhu
Vazhi koduthai
Vizhi vandha pinnaal yaen ?
Siragodithaai (2)

Thaththi sellum
Muthu sirppam
Kannukulle kanneer vetpam
Innum enna nenjil acham
Kannil mattum jeevan micham

Mullai poovil mullum undo
Kandu kollum indha veshamenna
Raja deepame ...

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the meaning of the song?

Sangeetha Jaathi Mullai expresses the beauty and depth of love through its poetic lyrics, reflecting on the essence of music and romance.

What is the plot context of the movie Kaadhal Oviyam?

The movie revolves around themes of love and relationships, showcasing the emotional journey of the characters intertwined with beautiful melodies.

Who is the singer of this song?

The song is sung by the legendary S. P. Balasubrahmanyam, known for his soulful voice and emotive singing style.

Who composed the music for this song?

The music for Sangeetha Jaathi Mullai was composed by the iconic Ilaiyaraaja, a maestro in the Tamil music industry.

When was this song released?

Sangeetha Jaathi Mullai was released on April 30, 1982, and has since become a classic.
enrichedMetadata.summary: In Sangeetha Jaathi Mullai, the song beautifully encapsulates the theme of love and longing, resonating with the emotional depth of the characters in Kaadhal Oviyam. The soothing melody, paired with heartfelt lyrics, evokes a sense of nostalgia and romance. It conveys the idea that music is an integral part of love, enhancing the emotional experience. This classic piece remains a cherished favorite among fans of Tamil cinema.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Sangeetha Jaathi Mullai from Kaadhal Oviyam is a timeless melody that captures the essence of love and longing. With the enchanting voice of S. P. Balasubrahmanyam and the masterful composition by Ilaiyaraaja, this song resonates deeply with listeners. Experience the magic of nostalgia as the lyrics weave a beautiful tapestry of emotions.
stanzaMeanings: The music of the jasmine flower cannot be seen. Even when the eyes come, there is no sight without the maiden.
Without melodies, there is no music. Our love is not complete. Come, my dear...
The music of the jasmine flower cannot be seen.
Does the face come and fade away? Does the wise face reveal itself? Do the tears flow in the eyes? Can it bear the sweetness?
In dreams, my life becomes a bond. Today, in the light, it cries in separation. In solitude, my heart will melt.
Even if the days are given, they cannot be embraced. Even if the days are given, they do not stay on the path.
Golden river, maiden river, life river.
As the eyes shed tears, the hands bow in prayer. The curls will fall, and a little rain of music will come. Will my peacock come now?
Will the pain in memory be resolved? Will the songs of love be sung? Will they be sung? Will they be sung? Will they be sung?
O king, will you come to my doorstep? O cuckoo... cuckoo... cuckoo... cuckoo...
Your melody stays in my heart and dances, O king.
I came searching for you, the moon of the season. You are the one who gives many poems to my heart. You are the one. When the eyes are not there, why did you give the path? After the eyes came, why?
Everywhere in my heart is your happiness. The sound of the waves is your fragrance.
The pearl that the father speaks of is a beautiful sculpture. In the eyes, there are tears of joy. What more fear is there in my heart? Life is only a fear in the eyes.
Is there jasmine in the jasmine garden? What is this disguise? O king...
